items.note: Confirmed benched vs Crystal Palace. Arsenal made wholesale changes ahead of Champions League final vs PSG. Youngsters Salmon and Dowman given starts. Saka worked individually Thu and was rested.

Left out of Man City matchday squad entirely for final day vs Aston Villa. Guardiola made 9 changes in his farewell game. Haaland joining Norway World Cup squad was a factor. Despite this, secured Golden Boot (27 goals).

Arsenal rotated heavily vs Crystal Palace ahead of CL final. Rice status unclear for GW38 — confirmed starter in CL final lineup but GW38 start not confirmed. High rotation risk given wholesale Arsenal changes.
Expected to lead Arsenal backline vs Crystal Palace despite rotation. Started Champions League final. Arteta noted Gabriel as penalty taker in CL final — key player trusted to feature GW38.
Nailed on starter. West Ham needed a result to avoid relegation. Bowen on penalties, plays 90 minutes. No rotation risk — survival stakes guarantee full commitment.

Top xPts player in squad (9.03). No injury flag. Nottingham Forest had nothing to rest for — no European final. Anderson expected to start. Transfer speculation (Man City bid rejected) does not affect GW38 availability.
Confirmed starter vs Brighton GW38. Scored in the match, broke a record. Man Utd won 3-0. Full 90 minutes expected.
Confirmed starter in goal for Brighton vs Man Utd. Made smart stops though Brighton lost 0-3.
Man City made 9 changes for Guardiola farewell. O'Reilly's starting status uncertain. Squad heavily rotated across the board.
No injury flag for Thiaw. Schaar (ankle) and Livramento (thigh) both out, meaning Thiaw's place in the backline is more secure, not less.
No injury flag in squad. Brentford had no major stakes — mid-table finish secured. Low rotation risk but no confirmed lineup data sourced. xPts only 2.28.
xPts null — no data calculated. Bournemouth unbeaten in 17 games, playing at full strength in GW38 with European qualification implications. Christie suspended. Hill's status not confirmed.
